free_text: States excited in 146Nd by thermal and average-
resonance neutron-capture gamma rays.
Internal target facility at Argonne
CP-5 research reactor.
Reactor core with a thermal-neutron flux ~3E+13
n/(cm2.s).
A 4-cm3 cooled intrinsic-germanium detector
located in a Compton-suppression pair spectrometer,
used in both coincidence and anticoincidence modes.
Energy and intensity calibrations based primarily on
the known lines from the 14N(n,g)15N reaction.
Calibration spectrum collected with target in place
and nitrogen gas used to flush the through tube.
Capture state (neutron separation) energy of 146Nd
determined to be 7564.7(2) keV.
Data taken from Table III, IV, and V in reference.
IS
Reactor spectrum
Three targets used.
. Two neodymium oxide targets, about 50 mg, contained
in small graphite holders. One target enriched to
89.67% in 145Nd, the other enriched to 91.06% in
. Natural neodymium target (8.3% 145Nd), about 25 g.
The spectrometer used in the coincidence mode
which allowed only double escape events to be
recorded.
Isotopic identifications made by comparison of thermal-
neutron-capture gamma-ray spectra from the target
enriched in 143Nd, from the target enriched in 145Nd,
and from the natural target.
Intensity uncertainties.
Data primarily taken
from this experiment.
Reactor spectrum effectively limited by boron that
removes low-energy neutrons. Dominant contributions to
spectrum were from neutrons in the approximate energy
range  200-500 eV.
Natural neodymium target (8.3% 145Nd), about 25 g,
surrounded by 0.95-cm thick boron absorber.
Average-resonance neutron-capture gamma-ray
experiment. Spectra obtained with the spectrometer
system in the coincidence mode which allowed only
double escape events to be recorded.
Capture averaged over many neutron resonances in order
to average out Porter-Thomas fluctuations in the
intensities of primary transitions resulting from
capture in a single resonance.
Intensity uncertainties.
Reduced relative intensities, i.e., measured
intensities multiplied by a factor (E0/E)**3, where
E0=7564.7 keV is the capture state energy and E is the
gamma-ray energy.
Average-resonance neutron-capture technique.
Reactor spectrum
Three targets used.
. Two neodymium oxide targets, about 50 mg, contained
in small graphite holders. One target enriched to
89.67% in 145Nd, the other enriched to 91.06% in
. Natural neodymium target (8.3% 145Nd), about 25 g.
Investigated secondary gamma transitions in the energy
range from 400 to 2600 keV by rejection of all but
full-energy detector events (anticoincidence mode).
Isotopic identifications made by comparison of thermal-
neutron-capture gamma-ray spectra from the target
enriched in 143Nd, from the target enriched in 145Nd,
and from the natural target.
Intensity uncertainties.
Contributions to relative intensity error due to errors
in the linewidth, efficiency, and transmission were at
most 2%, 10%, and 15%, respectively, up to 1500 keV.
Above 1500 keV the efficiency error was at most 20%.
